result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
The current bibliography only contains Sculley et al. (2015), which effectively covers high-level systemic and engineering challenges (Categories 1 & 5). To adequately address the workshop's focus on real-world deep learning failures, I must now incorporate literature on specific model failure modes, robustness limitations, and the disconnect between benchmark performance and real-world generalization. The most critical missing citation is a foundational paper that explains why deep models fail unexpectedly under distribution shift or adversarial conditions, specifically focusing on the reliance on non-robust features and shortcut learning. This will directly support discussions on specific theoretical concepts (Category 2), enable comparison of benchmark vs. real-world findings (Category 4), and provide empirical backing for arguments about model brittleness (Category 7). I will target the seminal work that reframes adversarial vulnerabilities as an inherent property of learn
